rag_rules: Preserve the existing AADL structure and insert annex content only within the owning component or implementation scope.
Do not move structural elements; add contract text without changing features, subcomponents, connections, or properties.
Keep scope consistent with the generated content: component-type contract material belongs in type scope, while implementation-only bindings belong in implementation scope.
Rely on the analyzed model as the authority for valid owners and available visible names before fusion.
generic_rules: Fuse generated content into the correct owner while preserving all original declarations and formatting as much as possible.
Avoid introducing references that are not valid in the selected owner scope.
If the generated content appears mismatched to owner scope, preserve architecture and defer structural changes rather than forcing insertion.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE content is wrapped in an `annex agree {** ... **};` block.
Implementation-scope annex content should appear in the implementation body after relevant structural declarations when such scope-dependent content exists.
anti_patterns: Do not insert type-scope contract content into an implementation owner or implementation-only content into a type owner.
Do not alter component names, ports, subcomponents, connections, or property associations during fusion.
irrelevant_topics: Requirement range semantics beyond owner/scope concerns
AADL property expressions as AGREE variables
